title: GitLab Webhook Consumer: Events â†’ sync_outbox Ingest
description:
GitLab-Webhook-Handler schreibt Events als `direction='inbound'` in `sync_outbox`.

**Dateien:**
- `app/services/gitlab_webhook_handler.py` â€” Event-Normalisierung + Mapping
- Erweiterung von `app/routers/webhooks.py` (bestehender generischer Ingest-Router)

**Auth:**
- Personal Access Token (PAT) via `HIVEMIND_GITLAB_TOKEN` Env-Var
- Alternativ: verschlĂĽsselt in app_settings (AES-256-GCM, selbes Pattern wie AI-Key)
- GitLab Webhook Secret Token: Validierung ĂĽber `X-Gitlab-Token` Header

**Event-Mapping:**
| GitLab Event | Hivemind event_type |
|---|---|
| `Issue Hook` (opened/reopened) | `gitlab_issue_opened` |
| `Merge Request Hook` (merged) | `gitlab_mr_merged` |
| `Pipeline Hook` (failed) | `gitlab_pipeline_failed` |
| `Push Hook` | `gitlab_push` |

**Normalisierung:**
- `normalize_gitlab_event(headers, payload) -> NormalizedEvent`
- NormalizedEvent: `{source, event_type, entity_type, entity_id, title, url, raw_payload}`
- Schreibt in sync_outbox: `direction='inbound', system='gitlab', routing_state='unrouted'`

**MCP Read-Tools:**
- `hivemind/get_gitlab_mr` â€” MR-Details via GitLab API abrufen
- `hivemind/get_gitlab_pipeline` â€” Pipeline-Status via GitLab API
- Beide read-only, fĂĽr Worker/Architekt-Kontext
- Auth: `HIVEMIND_GITLAB_TOKEN` als Bearer

**Env-Vars:** `HIVEMIND_GITLAB_TOKEN`, `HIVEMIND_GITLAB_URL` (Default: `https://gitlab.com`)

criteria: GitLab-Webhooks werden korrekt empfangen und X-Gitlab-Token validiert
Events werden normalisiert und in sync_outbox (direction='inbound') geschrieben
